statement: Never use synchronous filesystem operations (`fs.readFileSync`, `fs.writeFileSync`, `fs.statSync`) inside a request handler.
because: Node.js runs request handlers on the event loop. A synchronous fs call blocks the entire event loop — every other in-flight request waits. On a server handling 1,000 req/s, a single 50ms sync read becomes a 50-second tail-latency spike across all concurrent users.
if_violated: P99 latency degrades unpredictably under load. The degradation is invisible in development (only one request at a time). It surfaces in production as 'the server is slow' with no obvious cause in any single trace.
instead: Use the async equivalent: `await fs.promises.readFile(...)`. For configuration loaded at boot, sync is fine — boot is not a hot path. For anything per-request, async only.
